ksa_text: Knowledge of reference data management principles, including reference data taxonomy design, governance of controlled vocabularies, change management for reference data updates, and maintaining consistency across reference datasets.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of collection systems, capabilities, and processes.

nice.top_element_label: Identify the types of information to be processed, stored, or transmitted by a system

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Identify the types of information to be processed, stored, or transmitted by a system
Knowledge of collection systems, capabilities, and processes.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Data Quality falls outside the primary competency scope of the Director of AI/ML. This knowledge KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Data Quality truly out-of-scope for the Director of AI/ML? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Data Quality as a core or recommended competency for the Director of AI/ML. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Data Quality falls outside the established competency profile for the Director of AI/ML. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Data Quality is outside the competency scope of the Director of AI/ML. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
